key_insight: CatRetriever learns a shared latent space that maps catalyst slabs to plausible parent bulk crystals, enabling retrieval-based slab-to-bulk linking for generative catalyst discovery.
engineering_relevance: This lets catalyst design workflows evaluate bulk-dependent properties and synthesizability, not just surface structure, making generative screening more physically actionable.
method: Contrastive representation learning aligns slab and bulk crystal embeddings in one shared space.
Given a slab query, the model retrieves candidate parent bulks via nearest-neighbor search.
The framework is extended to target adsorption-energy bulk discovery by combining retrieval, search-space expansion, and adsorption-energy distribution analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong retrieval performance with R@1 above 91% and R@3 above 98% on both in-distribution and holdout test sets.
watch_item: Retrieval quality depends on the training library and may still produce structurally plausible but experimentally unsynthesizable candidates, so downstream validation remains necessary.
paper_title: CatRetriever: Contrastive Representation Learning for Slab-to-Bulk Retrieval in Generative Catalyst Discovery
